tickets.title: NEW-G — UAT case rollup from acceptance criteria + UAT canvas findings
spec: **Acceptance criteria** — parsed from the ticket description's `## Acceptance` section. Each bullet becomes a UAT case. Status: `pending` until manually marked or until a finding closes it.

**UAT canvas findings** — when a UAT session classifies a finding against this ticket (Wave 4.5 territory, see [STO-2176](https://linear.app/stonekey/issue/STO-2176/uat-canvas-question-card-classified-findings-list)), it lands here as `fail` or `pending` depending on resolution.

**Manually authored UAT cases** — added via the `Generate tests` action on the inline expand (NEW-E) when Claude is asked to author them; persisted in `.atrium/uat-cases.yaml` (or similar) co-located with the project.

tickets.title: W0.6-1 — Board snapshot source + BoardSource loader (replace STUB_WAVES)
spec: `extension/src/atrium-board.json` — committed snapshot of the real Atrium board, generated by a Claude session via the Linear MCP. Schema: `project`, `generatedAt`, `spikes[]`, `waves[]` (name, label, stage, passN, gatedBy, tickets\[\]), each ticket = `id, title, url, priority, state, spec[], tests{passed,failed,missing,discovered}, activity[]`.

`extension/src/board.ts` — pure module (no `vscode` import, mirrors the `cockpit-html.ts` split): `interface BoardSource { load(): Board }`; `SnapshotSource` imports the JSON; `LinearSdkSource` is a stub for the live path.
`extension.ts` `buildInitPayload()` reads from `board.ts` instead of `STUB_WAVES`.

tickets.title: Spike: how does the cockpit know something is "in testing"? (and is it worth it)
spec: A Linear **workflow state** — is "In Review" enough, or is there a dedicated "QA"/"Testing"/"UAT" state to map?
A **UAT label** on tickets/waves?
**Test-run results** (would need running the suite — likely too heavy for a passive cockpit)?

tickets.title: UAT / testing cycle — how the flow represents build → test → release
spec: Where testing/UAT sits in the pipeline (Plan → Build → **UAT/test** → Release) and how a wave reads while it's being tested.
Loop-back: a wave that fails UAT returns to Build/Design (Pass-N chip + return-strip already built).
Decide whether to revive the parked `UatView` (classified findings composer → Linear comment) as the testing surface, or keep it lighter.

tickets.title: End-to-end dogfood — run Atrium start→finish on a real in-flight wave

Have a ticket genuinely in flight while doing it, to see how the live board + sprint behave.
Log every friction point.

tickets.title: Plan canvas — render the PRD + make it editable / reorderable
spec: Render the wave's PRD doc (resolved via file-discovery) in the Plan canvas.

Requirements stay as Linear tickets + the repo PRD doc; Atrium surfaces + reorders, doesn't replace Linear.

tickets.title: Design canvas — render mockups live (preview) + edit affordance
spec: Live-preview HTML mockups in a sandboxed iframe inside the webview (look good).
"Open to edit in VS Code" / "ask Claude to edit" affordance — editing happens on the **repo files** (agent or user); Atrium re-renders on change.
Render PRD/spec markdown nicely where relevant.

tickets.title: File discovery — how Atrium finds the PRD + mockups for a wave/sprint
spec: Naming convention (e.g. `docs/superpowers/specs/<wave>.md`, `files/<wave>-*.html`)?
Markdown frontmatter tagging a file to a wave?
A small per-project manifest (`.atrium/waves.json`)?

tickets.title: Conditional Design / UX stages — only enacted when there's design work
spec: **When are Design/UX enacted per wave?** Options: a `Design`/`UX` signal (label), the presence of mockup/design files for the wave (ties to file-discovery), or a manual per-wave toggle.
Default flow with no design = **Plan → Build → (UAT) → Release**; Design/UX light up only when the wave has design scope.
Pipeline only marks Design/UX active when the wave genuinely has design scope; no empty forced stages.

tickets.title: Auto-derive wave pipeline stage from ticket states
spec: No ticket started → **Plan**
The second any ticket is in progress / in review → **Build**
All tickets done → **Release**

tickets.title: Provider portability — formalize a BoardWriter interface + Jira adapter spike

Spike a **Jira adapter**: map Jira status-categories / labels-or-components / rank / **Sprints** (native — maps better than Linear cycles) → the cockpit model.
Document the adapter contract so a fork can implement a new provider against it.
